svg.title: Corporate structure vs standard business operations vs current Lados structure, with a proposed redesign
svg.desc: Three comparisons: (1) the textbook org-structure hierarchy (Organization, Department, Team) alongside the textbook business-operations hierarchy (Program, Project, Workflow, Task); (2) how Lados is built today, where Pipeline incorrectly sits beside Project instead of above it; (3) a proposed redesign renaming Pipeline to Program and nesting Projects inside it as children, matching standard operations.
svg.text: Corporate Structure · Business Operations · Lados Today
Comparing the textbook model to what's built, and what a redesign would look like
Program = one business goal, delivered through several Projects, separated by Stage Gates (go / no-go reviews).
Lados today — Pipeline sits beside Project, not above it
⚠ Pipeline sits beside Project, not above it
Both Project and Pipeline hang directly off Organization today — no parent/child link between them in the database.
Proposed redesign — Program becomes the parent
Program owns its Projects directly — they're children, not peers.
Same underlying data as today — only where Pipeline sits in the tree changes.
Renames to match standard operations language
Node → Task (business-facing label; canvas can keep "node" internally)
Automated stage (a Project/Workflow running on its own)
Structural mismatch in today's design (Section 2 only)
